intro: Hymenostachys trollii, also known as Tibetan club moss, is a type of lycopod plant that belongs to the Hymenostachys genus. It has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in treating conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hymenostachys trollii include triterpenoid saponins, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hymenostachys trollii is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used to treat coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory ailments.
